claim: The May 25 public snapshot supersedes the May 24 snapshot by fix-forward (a new dated snapshot, not an in-place re-cut) and uses the US CDC Current Situation page (25 May) as the highest valid primary on the latest date for suspected, confirmed, and deaths. The endpoint takes the higher valid primary and does not average across sources or down-revise to a lower peer.

ECDC 25 May reports 101 confirmed, 904 suspected, and 119 suspected deaths. The DRC MoH 24 May dashboard aggregate reports 854 reported cases, 112 confirmed DRC cases, and 179 registered deaths. The reconciled endpoint takes CDC's 906 suspected, 112 confirmed, and 223 deaths; the lower or source-review figures 179, 119, and 177 are dated conflict anchors.

claim: Promote INRB SitRep #016 (data cutoff 2026-05-30, published 2026-05-31) to the headline-count endpoint for the May-30 publication-state snapshot. SitRep #016 introduces a refined schema that bifurcates confirmed cases into cumulative (282) and active (238 = 282 - 42 deaths - 2 cured), and alongside the cumulative tiles reports an operational point-prevalence split: cas suspects en cours d'investigation (220) + cas suspects en isolement (101) = an active operational caseload of 321 at the 2026-05-30 cutoff. This operational caseload sits on a separate operational-status axis: it is a point-in-time stock, national-only, not a cumulative case count, and is never summed into the confirmed cumulative count. The cumul-cas-confirmes 282 tile carries an asterisk footnote 'donnees en cours d'harmonisation'. The cumul-cas-suspects (cumulative) and cumul-deces-suspects tiles are NOT included; their May-26 values are retained only as dated archival conflict anchors, never summed into confirmed. Country-scope composition continues to add the ECDC 27 May Uganda anchor (7 confirmed, 1 confirmed death).
value: Country-scope confirmed = 282 DRC + 7 UGA = 289. Country-scope confirmed-active = 238 DRC + 7 UGA = 245. Operational point-prevalence caseload (operational-status axis, not cumulative, never summed into confirmed) = 321 (DRC, no Uganda operational anchor): 220 under investigation + 101 in isolation at the 2026-05-30 cutoff. Cumulative-suspected May-26 baseline (349) is retained only as a dated archival conflict anchor. Country-scope confirmed deaths = 42 DRC + 1 UGA = 43. Recovered (gueris) = 2. Contact-follow-up rate = 45.2% (DRC, new tile).

claim: Primary modeled burden = 4708 total infections (1779 confirmed; about 2929 unreported; about 38% ascertainment). Death-anchored stress = 7257 total infections (6020 to 8747; about 5478 unreported if the death anchor is taken literally). Imperial Method 2 external cross-check = 4110 to 6323 at deaths 602; not the primary burden estimate. The onset-to-death growth correction is taken from the Rosello 2015 BDBV gamma and the CFR from the US CDC prior-outbreak aggregate; the implementation is original code.

claim: INRB conducted an investigation-driven revision of the cumulative-suspected case series between May 26 and May 28. The pre-revision count (1077) included unscreened alerts; the post-revision count (349) is the cumulative residual after investigation cleared a portion as non-cases and reclassified others as confirmed. The revision is documented in SitRep #014 with footnote 'Revised downward; number of suspect cases was revised down after investigation and sampling confirmed some and ruled out others'. We treat the May-26 1077 as superseded (it is no longer the upstream's current cumulative); the post-revision 349 becomes the new baseline. SitRep #015 continued at 349 with a superscript footnote marker. SitRep #016 dropped the cumulative tile entirely and replaced it with an active-stock split (220 under investigation + 101 in isolation = 321 active); the cumulative carries forward at the field level with reason source_schema_evolved.
value: Operating condition (not a snapshot value): INRB retains the right to refine the suspected case definition mid-outbreak. This non-monotonic behaviour (1077 then 349) is one of the reasons the cumulative-suspected tier was retired from our cumulative surface: a cumulative incidence count cannot fall by two-thirds and remain interpretable as incidence. Both the pre-revision 1077 and the post-revision 349 are retained here only as dated archival conflict anchors documenting the lineage; neither is published as a cumulative metric and neither is summed into confirmed. Going forward only confirmed and confirmed_deaths are cumulative; suspected cases pending classification are reported on the separate operational-status axis (under investigation and in isolation) as point-prevalence.
source_refs: Centre d'Operations d'Urgence de Sante Publique (COUSP RDC), INSP, INRB, MoH DRC. SitRep N°014/MVB_28/2026 (data cutoff 28 May, published 29 May).; SitRep #015 (29 May cutoff, 30 May published).
public_action: Treat any future INRB suspected-series revision as an operating condition, not an error: keep the superseded figure as a dated conflict anchor and re-baseline going forward.
public_note: Detailed audit IDs and review locators are withheld from this public export.
